rows:
Works by any public
authority acting
pursuant to the
provisions of any Act
on, in, over or under a
public street, or works
for a utility service.

Works urgently
necessary for public
safety, safety or
security of plant or
equipment,
maintenance of
essential services, or
protection of the
environment.

Temporary works in
existence for less than
14 days or such longer
time as the City agrees.
The works are not signs or advertisements.

The carrying out of any
building or works to a
building which affects
only the interior of a
building on the Heritage
List.

a) The works are confined to:
i. Existing kitchen, bathroom or
laundry fit out with no structural
alterations;
ii. Replacement of light fitting(s);
iii. Painting/wall papering/plastering
of internal walls*;
iv. Retiling;
v. Construction of new internal non-
masonry, non-load bearing walls;
vi. New floor covering placed over but
not replacing or damaging existing
floor surface materials; or
vii. Electrical and plumbing works.
b) The place is not:
i. Level 1A or 1B on the local
Heritage List;
ii. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
iii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

The carrying out of any
building or works to a

building which affects
only the interior of a
building in a Heritage
Area.

i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List
unless exempt under No. 4 above.

The demolition of a
building on a lot to
which the R-Codes do
not apply.
i. entered in the State Register of
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
iii. included on the Heritage List; or
iv. included in a Heritage Area.

a) If the building is an outbuilding:
i. it is not of masonry or limestone
construction; and
ii. has a floor area of less than 25m2.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included on the Heritage List; or
iv. included in a Heritage Area.

Demolition of internal
and boundary / dividing
fences in Heritage Areas
or Level 3 on the
Heritage List.

a) The fence is not limestone or masonry.
b) The fence is not on the primary or
secondary street(s).
c) The place is not:
i. Level 2, 1A or 1B on the Heritage
List;
ii. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
iii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.
A change in the use of

Note that works to a heritage-protected
place will still require planning approval.

A change in the use of
an existing building to
any of the following
uses:
i. Shop;
ii. Office; or
iii. Consulting
rooms.

If the use is a shop, the net lettable area
does not exceed 300m2.

A change of use of a
building which currently
has Development
Approval for use as a
Small Bar to use as a
Restaurant.

The new use operates in accordance with
any conditions of Development Approval
relating to the hours of operation that
apply to the currently approved Small Bar
use on the subject site.
Note that works to a heritage-protected
place will still require planning approval.

Patios, carports, shade
structures and awnings
to non-residential
development.

a) Patios, carports and shade sails have a
maximum height of 3.5m.
b) The development is outside of the
primary and secondary street setback
area(s).
c) There is a minimum 1m setback to any
residential zoned land.
d) The development does not reduce the
landscaping or tree supply required by
a local planning policy.
e) The place is not a heritage-protected
place.
Additions and Alterations to Single Houses and Grouped Dwellings

Additions and
Alterations to Single
Houses or Grouped
Dwellings identified as
‘Contributory’ in a
Heritage Area.
See Note 1 above
a) The works are single storey (ground
b) The works are located between a
dwelling and the side or rear lot
i. Are lower in height than the place;
ii. Are set back a minimum of 1m
iii. Have a new roof independent from
the roof form of the place and
of the roof of the original building
iv. Have no alteration to existing roof
e) The works meet the applicable
i. entered in the State Register of

ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included on the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
5.7 (SCA 5.7) and with a
development granted approval
under clause 5.7 of Local Planning
Scheme No. 4.

Additions and
alterations to all Single
Houses and Grouped
Dwellings, including
those in a Heritage Area
that are not
‘Contributory’.
See Note 1 above

a) The works are single storey (ground
level).
b) The works are located between a
dwelling and the side or rear lot
boundary of a development site
(excluding secondary street setback
areas).
c) The works meet the applicable
deemed-to-comply provisions of the
Residential Design Codes and any
relevant local planning policy.
d) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included on the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
5.7 (SCA 5.7) and with a
development granted approval
under clause 5.7 of Local Planning
Scheme No. 4.

The modification of
major and minor
openings to an existing
Single House or
Grouped Dwelling.

a) The modifications meet the relevant
deemed-to-comply provisions of the
Residential Design Codes.
b) If the building is identified as a
‘Contributory’ property in a Heritage
Area, the openings do not impact the
original building exterior (See Note 1
above).
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.
iii. included on the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
Outbuildings, Patios and Shade Structures to Heritage-protected Places

Small outbuildings in
Heritage Areas and/or a
place listed as Level 3
on the City’s Heritage
List.

a) The outbuilding is located between a
dwelling and a rear lot boundary of a
development site (excluding secondary
street setback areas).
b) The outbuilding meets the applicable
deemed-to-comply provisions of the
Residential Design Codes for a small
outbuilding.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included as a level 1A, 1B or 2 on
the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
5.7 (SCA 5.7) and with a
development granted approval
under clause 5.7 of Local Planning
Scheme No. 4.

Patios to a Single house
or Grouped dwelling
listed as Level 3 on the
City’s Heritage List.

a) The works are located between a
dwelling and the rear lot boundary of a
development site (excluding secondary
street setback areas).
b) Maximum height of 3m.
c) The works meet the applicable
deemed-to-comply provisions of the
Residential Design Codes.
d) The works are freestanding or do not
include removal or alterations to the
place aside from wall fixings.
e) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included as a level 1A, 1B or 2 on
the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
5.7 (SCA 5.7) and with a
development granted approval
under clause 5.7 of Local Planning
Scheme No. 4.

Shade structures to a
Single house or Grouped

a) The structure is located between a
dwelling and the rear lot boundary of a

dwelling in Heritage
Areas or listed as Level
3 on the City’s Heritage
List.

development site (excluding secondary
street setback areas).
b) The structure is no greater than 3
metres in height and 25m2 in area.
c) The works are freestanding or do not
include removal or alterations to the
place aside from wall fixings.
d) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement;
iii. included as a level 1A, 1B or 2 on
the Heritage List; or
iv. located within Special Control Area
5.7 (SCA 5.7) and with a
development granted approval
under clause 5.7 of Local Planning
Scheme No. 4.

Fences on or abutting a
property on the
Heritage List and/or
within a Heritage Area.

a) The fence is not located within the
primary or secondary street.
b) The maximum height is 1.2m where
within 3m of the boundary of any local
open space or regional reserve for
parks and recreation, and a maximum
height of 1.8m in all other cases.
c) The fence is in line with or behind the
main wall of the building when viewed
from both the primary and secondary
streets.
d) The fence is freestanding and not
attached to any existing structure,
existing building or heritage feature.
e) If the fence is subject to the
Residential Design Codes, it meets the
deemed-to-comply criteria for vehicle
sightlines.
f) If the fence is not subject to the
Residential Design Codes, it meets the
vehicle sightlines criteria of the
relevant Australian Standards.
g) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or

ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

Primary and secondary
street fences in a
Heritage Area
(excluding the Hilton
Heritage Area).

a) Fencing in the primary or secondary
street setback areas (forward of the
main wall of the place and measured
from the street elevation) is:
i. Solid up to 0.9m in height; or
ii. Picket, post and wire or slat fences
up to 1.2m in height and visually
permeable; or
iii. Hybrid with a solid portion up to
0.5m and visually permeable
above to a total of 1.2m in height;
and
iv. With any piers up to 1.5m in
height.
b) Fences meet the deemed-to-comply
criteria of the R-Codes for vehicle
sightlines.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List

Primary and secondary
street fences in the
Hilton Heritage Area

a) Street walls and fences forward of the
building line are a maximum height of
1.5m as measured from the street
elevation, and with the following visual
permeability:
Max Height Minimum Visual
Permeability
Up to 0.9m 20% above 0.3m
1m to 1.2m 50% above 0.3m
1.3m to 1.5m 85% above 0.3m
b) Fences meet the deemed-to-comply
criteria of the R-Codes for vehicle
sightlines.

Screening material
attached to an existing
dividing fence.

a) The material projects no more than
500mm above the top of the fence at
any point.
b) The material is not situated within a
primary or secondary street setback
area.

Private swimming pools
and outdoor spas in
Heritage Areas.

a) The works are situated between a
dwelling and the rear lot boundary of a
development site, and no greater than
1.8m in height.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.
Note that pool fencing is subject to
planning approval unless otherwise exempt
in this Policy.

Filling or excavation of
land with no more than
500mm change to the
natural ground level.

a) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.
b) The works do not impact a regulated
tree as defined in Local Planning Policy
2.26: Tree Retention.

Construction of a
retaining wall less than
500mm in height above
natural ground level.

a) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.
b) The works do not impact a regulated
tree as defined in Local Planning Policy
2.26: Tree Retention.

a) The finished level of the surface is no
more than 500mm above natural
ground level.
b) The works meet the deemed-to-
comply landscaping provisions of the
R-Codes or a relevant local planning
policy such as LPP3.8: O’Connor.

c) If the outdoor hard surface is located
within a heritage-protected place, the
works are located between a dwelling
and the side or rear lot boundary of a
development site (excluding secondary
street setback areas).
d) The works do not impact a regulated
tree as defined in Local Planning Policy
2.26: Tree Retention.
e) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

The painting or
application of render on
the external surface of
any building or
structure.

a) If the building is in a Heritage Area,
the works are not to the original
building exterior.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.

The maintenance and
repair of any building or
structure being lawfully
used immediately prior
to the Scheme having
effect.

The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

Replacing or altering
roofing materials on a
building.

a) There is no change to the roof form or
pitch.
b) If the building is within a Heritage
Area, the works are:
i. not to a contributory place (See
Note 1 above); or
ii. not impacting the original building
exterior; or
iii. replacement of asbestos with
corrugated zincalume or
galvanised steel sheeting; or
iv. like-for-like material replacement.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.

Adding, altering or
replacing external
cladding materials on a
building.

a) There is no change to the building
form.
b) If the building is within a Heritage
Area, the works are not to the original
building exterior.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.
Air Conditioners, Solar Panels and Other External Fixtures

External fixtures
installed on a building to
which the R-Codes do
not apply.

a) If the building is within a Heritage
Area, the works are not visible from
the street.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. included on the Heritage List.

External fixtures
(excluding air
conditioners) on a place
on the Heritage List.

a) The external fixtures:
i. do not exceed 1.8m in height and
are fixed to a wall of a building
which faces the rear or a side
boundary (excluding walls facing
secondary streets); or

ii. are fixed to a plane of the roof of
a building which faces the rear or
a side boundary (excluding roof
planes facing secondary streets),
or to a flat roof, and project no
more than 1m above the part of
the existing roof to which the
fixture is attached and no greater
than 2m wide, or projecting no
more than 2m above the highest
part of the existing roof at any
point in the case of an aerial or
antenna.
b) If the fixture is a satellite antenna or
dish, the maximum diameter is 1m.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

External fixtures
(excluding air
conditioners) in a
Heritage Area.

a) The external fixtures:
i. do not exceed 1.8m in height and
are fixed to a wall of a building
which faces the rear or a side
boundary (excluding walls facing
secondary streets); or
ii. are fixed to a plane of the roof of
a building which faces the rear or
a side boundary (excluding roof
planes facing secondary streets),
or to a flat roof, and project no
more than 1m above the part of
the existing roof to which the
fixture is attached, or projecting
no more than 2m above the
highest part of the existing roof at
any point in the case of an aerial
or antenna.
b) If the fixture is a satellite antenna or
dish, the maximum diameter is 1m.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement; or
iii. on the Heritage List.

a) The unit:
i. does not exceed 1.8m in height
and is fixed to a wall of a building
which faces a side boundary
(excluding walls facing secondary
streets);
ii. is fixed to a wall or roof plane of a
building which faces the rear
boundary at a height not
exceeding the highest part of the
existing roof at any point; or
iii. if the building has a flat roof and is
not on the Heritage List, is in a
position where the top of the air
conditioning unit is not more than
1m above the highest part of the
existing roof at any point.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order
or Agreement; or
iii. on the Heritage List.

Water tanks on a place
on the Heritage List or
in a Heritage Area.

a) The water tank:
i. is fixed to a wall of a building
which faces the rear or a side
boundary (excluding walls facing
secondary streets) at a height
not exceeding the eaves height;
or
ii. is free standing and located
between a dwelling and the side
or rear lot boundary of a
development site (excluding
secondary street setback areas),
and no greater than 2.4m in
height if more than 1m from any
boundary, or 1.8m in height if
less than 1m from any boundary.
b) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places;
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order
or Agreement; or
iii. on the Heritage List.

a) If within the primary street setback
area, the structure is a maximum of
2m in height, excluding clothes lines.
b) If outside of the primary street setback
area, the structure is a maximum of
3m in height.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

a) Free standing and a maximum of 6m
in height.
b) Not used for advertising purposes.
c) The place is not:
i. entered in the State Register of
Heritage Places; or
ii. the subject of a Heritage Order or
Agreement.

Removal of trees or
vegetation areas.

a) Trees or vegetation are not:
i. identified on the Register of
Significant Trees or Vegetation
Areas; or
ii. required to be retained on a site
through a condition of
Development Approval.
b) The works are exempt as set out in
Local Planning Policy 2.26: Tree
Retention.
Any change to the words, pictures, symbols
or colours of any approved advertisement
where the change does not alter the size or

• One sign per street frontage
advertising for sale, lease or rent the
property on which the sign is situated.
• Maximum area as follows—
o Sites less than 5000 sqm - 2 sqm
o Sites greater than 5000sqm - 5
sqm
displayed only for the duration of the
o Sites less than 5000 sqm — 2 sqm

• One sign per road frontage.
• Maximum area as follows—
o Sites less than 5000 sqm — 2 sqm
o Sites greater than 5000 sqm — 5
sqm

In all zones,
excluding the
Residential zone
• located on the lot to which the sign
relates and directly relating to the
goods, services, or functions of the
property on which it is located,
• does not pose a hazard or obstruction

In all zones,
excluding the
Residential zone

Provided the sign is/are displayed on the
windows of the business premises from
which the advertised item is sold, or the
advertised services are supplied, and the
total sign does not cover more than 50% of
the total window area of the tenancy as
viewed from the street.
On the site to which they pertain. Maximum

A single line of letters not exceeding
300mm in height and fixed to the façade of
the building.

Signs erected by a public
authority, or on its behalf
for the purpose of public
safety or information
and/or the direction and
control of people, animals
or vehicles

Development Exempt from
Planning Approval Under LPS4

Inclusion of COVID provisions and limitations
on Small Bars/Restaurants

Update to reflect new heritage areas policy and
incorporate fencing

Incorporate fence & sign exemptions; modify
exemptions for heritage places, grouped
dwellings and non-res land use; add definitions
